title: Deploy loopctl to Fly.io with managed PostgreSQL

story.i_want_to: deploy loopctl to Fly.io with a managed PostgreSQL database and automated CI/CD
story.so_that: the service is publicly accessible at loopctl.com with production-grade reliability, automatic deployments on merge to master, and no dependency on the self-hosted beelink server

rationale: loopctl is currently self-hosted on a beelink machine behind a residential network. To offer it as a public service, it needs to run on cloud infrastructure with proper SSL, managed database, health monitoring, and automated deploys. Fly.io is the proven platform — both home_care_billing and reportex already run there successfully with the same Phoenix/Elixir stack.

acceptance_criteria.description: fly.toml is updated from placeholder to production-ready: app name 'loopctl', primary_region 'lax', PHX_HOST set to 'loopctl.com', internal_port 8080, min_machines_running 1, health check on /health, force_https true.

Dockerfile is updated for Fly.io deployment: port 8080 (EXPOSE 8080, ENV PORT=8080), Node.js added to builder stage for esbuild/tailwind asset compilation, tini removed (Fly handles init), runtime deps match production needs.

Fly Managed PostgreSQL is provisioned. RLS roles (loopctl_app and loopctl_admin) are created via a one-time setup script run through 'fly postgres connect'. The setup script grants privileges and creates the current_tenant_id() function. This is documented in deploy/fly-db-setup.sh.

DATABASE_URL uses direct Postgres connection (port 5433, bypassing PgBouncer) to ensure SET LOCAL works correctly for RLS. ADMIN_DATABASE_URL uses the loopctl_admin role. Both are set as Fly secrets. The runtime.exs documents why direct connections are required.

Runtime config (config/runtime.exs) detects Fly.io environment via FLY_APP_NAME env var and applies: connect_timeout 15s, queue_target 5s, queue_interval 10s (matching reportex patterns). IPv6 enabled via ECTO_IPV6.
All required secrets are set via 'fly secrets set': DATABASE_URL, ADMIN_DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY_BASE, CLOAK_KEY. No secrets are hardcoded in fly.toml or Dockerfile.
Release command '/app/bin/migrate' runs Ecto migrations on deploy. The Ecto.Migrator runs with the admin role to create/modify RLS policies. Migrations succeed on a fresh database.
DNS is configured: loopctl.com points to the Fly.io app via CNAME. Fly.io issues and manages the SSL certificate via 'fly certs add loopctl.com'. HTTPS is enforced.
GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow adds a deploy job that runs on push to master after all quality gates pass. Deploy step uses 'flyctl deploy --remote-only'. FLY_API_TOKEN is stored as a GitHub secret.
Session affinity is enabled for LiveView WebSocket stability: sticky sessions with TTL 3600s, http2 backend disabled (h2_backend = false). Matches home_care_billing and reportex patterns.

After deployment: GET https://loopctl.com returns the landing page, GET https://loopctl.com/health returns JSON {status: ok}, GET https://loopctl.com/api/v1/ returns the API discovery JSON. All API endpoints are functional with RLS enforced.
The self-hosted beelink deployment (docker-compose) continues to work. The PORT env var (default 4000 for beelink, 8080 for Fly) is the only difference. No code changes break the existing setup.

test_cases.name: Health check endpoint returns ok on Fly.io
expected_results: Returns 200 with JSON {status: ok, database: connected}

test_cases.name: API routes are accessible and RLS is enforced
preconditions: Two tenants with API keys exist
steps: Create a project under tenant A via API
expected_results: Tenant B cannot see tenant A's project (RLS enforced)
SET LOCAL works correctly (not broken by PgBouncer)

test_cases.name: Landing page is served on Fly.io
preconditions: App is deployed with US-18.1 landing page

test_cases.name: HTTPS is enforced and certificate is valid
expected_results: HTTPS returns 200 with valid TLS certificate for loopctl.com

test_cases.name: Migrations run successfully on deploy
preconditions: Fresh Fly.io deployment with new database
expected_results: RLS policies exist on all tenant-scoped tables
Both loopctl_app and loopctl_admin roles exist and function

test_cases.name: CI/CD pipeline deploys on master push
preconditions: FLY_API_TOKEN is set as GitHub secret
expected_results: Workflow runs: lint → test → security → dialyzer → deploy
New version is live at loopctl.com within 5 minutes

technical_notes: Reference deployments: home_care_billing and reportex (fly.toml, Dockerfile, .github/workflows/ci.yml)

CRITICAL: Fly Managed Postgres uses PgBouncer in transaction mode by default. SET LOCAL (required for RLS) does NOT work through PgBouncer transaction mode. Use DIRECT connections on port 5433 (not 5432) to bypass PgBouncer entirely.

RLS role setup: Fly Managed Postgres only gives you one superuser. Create loopctl_app and loopctl_admin roles manually via 'fly postgres connect -a loopctl-db' then run deploy/fly-db-setup.sh. This is a one-time setup, not per-deploy.
DATABASE_URL format for direct connection: postgres://loopctl_app:PASSWORD@loopctl-db.flycast:5433/loopctl?sslmode=disable (port 5433 = direct, 5432 = PgBouncer)
ADMIN_DATABASE_URL: same but with loopctl_admin role
Dockerfile: add Node.js to builder stage for esbuild/tailwind asset compilation (was API-only before US-18.1)
fly.toml session affinity: [http_service.http_options] h2_backend = false; [http_service.session_affinity] = { type = 'by_header', ttl = 3600 }
GitHub Actions deploy step: uses superfly/flyctl-actions/setup-flyctl@master then 'flyctl deploy --remote-only'
DNS: fly certs add loopctl.com, then CNAME loopctl.com → loopctl.fly.dev at registrar
The beelink docker-compose setup stays as-is — both deployment targets share the same codebase, only PORT and DATABASE_URL differ
Oban: ensure webhook delivery worker has outbound HTTP access (Fly allows this by default)
Consider fly scale memory 1024 if 512MB is insufficient with LiveView + API traffic
